quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungary has more thermal springs than almost any other country in the world, with over 1,000 natural hot springs! Budapest alone has 118 natural thermal springs, which is why it's known as the 'City of Spas.' These healing waters have been enjoyed since Roman times.

short_read.title: The Secret Life of Hummingbirds in Your Garden
short_read.teaser: These tiny jewels can remember every flower they've visited and how long it takes each one to refill with nectar. Learn how these remarkable birds plan their daily routes like little aerial mathematicians.

medium_read.title: The Return of Sea Otters to the Pacific Northwest
medium_read.teaser: Once hunted nearly to extinction, sea otters are slowly returning to Washington's coastal waters. Their comeback story is not just heartwarming—these playful animals are essential to keeping kelp forests healthy and thriving.

long_read.title: The Ancient Art of Hungarian Embroidery
long_read.teaser: For centuries, Hungarian women have passed down intricate embroidery patterns that tell stories of their villages and families. This beautiful tradition connects modern Hungary to its folk roots through colorful threads and meaningful symbols.

port_angeles.title: Butterfly Season at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: Late July is peak butterfly season on Hurricane Ridge! The alpine meadows are alive with colorful species like the Anise Swallowtail and Painted Lady butterflies dancing among the wildflowers. It's a magical time to visit the high country and enjoy the cool mountain air.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That's incredibly fast—about 20 beats every second!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute—almost unbelievably fast!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: These tiny birds have mighty hearts! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute in flight—that's 20 times every second.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To support such a fast heart rate, hummingbirds must eat constantly, consuming about half their body weight in sugar daily. When they sleep at night, their heart rate drops to as low as 50 beats per minute to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm! That's perfect—you know your Hungarian!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word is 'köszönöm' (or the shorter 'köszi'). It's one of the most important words to know!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is unique among European languages—it's part of the Finno-Ugric language family, making it more closely related to Finnish and Estonian than to any of its neighboring countries' languages. This makes it one of the most distinctive languages in Europe.

puzzle_3.question: What do sea otters do while sleeping to avoid drifting apart from their group?
options: Anchor to rocks with their tails
Form a circle and link paws
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Sea otters hold hands while sleeping—it's called 'rafting' and it's as adorable as it sounds. This keeps the group together in the ocean currents.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Sea otters actually hold hands while they sleep! This sweet behavior, called 'rafting,' keeps them from drifting apart while floating on their backs.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: A group of resting sea otters is called a 'raft,' and can include just a few otters or over 100 animals. They also wrap themselves in kelp like a blanket to stay anchored in one spot while they rest.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: Sea Otter Facts — Monterey Bay Aquarium

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
